title: triticum vulgare subsp. spelta
intro: Triticum vulgare subsp. spelta, also known as spelt, is a subspecies of the common wheat plant (Triticum vulgare) that has been cultivated for thousands of years. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, which may help to soothe digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of menopause. The health benefits of spelt include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve blood sugar control, and support the immune system. Spelt can be prepared as an herbal tea, flour, or supplement, making it a versatile ingredient in traditional medicine and a popular choice for those seeking a natural, wheat-based alternative.
